2629:56939.name: variant=success
2629:56939.description:
variant: success
Light green background color with darker green text for positive messaging. Circular checkmark icon appears on the left side indicating successful completion or positive status. Used for confirmations, completed actions, or positive feedback messages.


Content Configuration Variations
hasIcon: trueVariant-appropriate icon displays on the left side of the alert, positioned vertically centered with the title text. Icon color matches the variant's text color for visual consistency. Provides immediate visual recognition of alert type without requiring text reading.


hasIcon: falseNo icon element present. Content area spans the full width of the alert container from left edge. Title and description text maintain proper margins and alignment without icon spacing considerations.


isDismissible: trueX button appears in the top-right corner of the alert, styled to match the variant's text color. Button is interactive and provides hover/focus states for accessibility. Clicking dismisses the alert from view.


isDismissible: falseNo dismiss button present. Alert remains persistent in interface until programmatically removed or user navigates away. Appropriate for critical messages requiring acknowledgment through other means.


hasTitle: trueBold title text appears as the first line of content, providing clear message hierarchy. Title uses variant-appropriate text color with increased font weight for prominence. Description text follows below with standard formatting.


hasTitle: falseOnly description text displays within the alert content area. Text styling remains consistent with variant but may receive slight emphasis since it serves as the primary message. Useful for simple, brief notifications.


hasLink: trueActionable link element included within the alert content area, typically appearing after the description text or integrated within the message. Link color coordinates with variant styling while maintaining sufficient contrast for accessibility.


hasLink: falseAlert contains only static text content without interactive elements beyond optional dismiss button. Appropriate for informational messages that don't require user action beyond acknowledgment.

2441:839.name: emphasis=inverted, state=default
2441:839.description:
emphasis: inverted
Light text styling designed for dark backgrounds or colored surfaces. Link text provides appropriate contrast against darker backgrounds while maintaining readability. Interactive states adapt to inverted color schemes.


state: default
Link displays in standard visual state with base styling appropriate to emphasis level. Text appears with normal opacity and contrast. Icons, when present, display with standard styling. No interaction feedback visible.


Content Configuration Variations
hasStartIcon: trueIcon element appears positioned before the link text with appropriate spacing. Icon styling matches the current emphasis level and interactive state. Icon participates in all hover, focus, and active state changes alongside the text.


hasStartIcon: falseNo start icon present. Link text begins at the natural starting position without additional spacing or icon considerations.


hasEndIcon: trueIcon element appears positioned after the link text with appropriate spacing. Icon styling coordinates with emphasis level and participates in interactive state changes. Common use cases include external link indicators or directional arrows.


hasEndIcon: falseNo end icon present. Link text ends at the natural completion position without trailing icon elements or additional spacing.

2629:56938.description:
variant: info
Light blue background color with darker blue text for informational content. Circular information (i) icon appears on the left side indicating neutral information or helpful tips. Used for system notifications, guidance, or explanatory messages.

hasLink: trueActionable link element included within the alert content area, typically appearing after the description text or integrated within the message. Link color coordinates with variant styling while maintaining sufficient contrast for accessibility.


hasLink: falseAlert contains only static text content without interactive elements beyond optional dismiss button. Appropriate for informational messages that don't require user action beyond acknowledgment.

2629:56937.description:
variant: warning
Light orange/yellow background color with darker orange text for cautionary messaging. Triangular warning icon with exclamation mark appears on the left side indicating potential issues or important notices. Used for non-blocking warnings or advisory messages.

hasLink: falseAlert contains only static text content without interactive elements beyond optional dismiss button. Appropriate for informational messages that don't require user action beyond acknowledgment.

2629:56936.name: variant=danger
2629:56936.description:
variant: dangerLight red/pink background color with darker red text for error or critical messaging. Circular exclamation icon appears on the left side indicating errors, failures, or critical issues. Used for error states, failed actions, or urgent notifications requiring immediate attention.


Content Configuration Variations
hasIcon: trueVariant-appropriate icon displays on the left side of the alert, positioned vertically centered with the title text. Icon color matches the variant's text color for visual consistency. Provides immediate visual recognition of alert type without requiring text reading.


hasIcon: falseNo icon element present. Content area spans the full width of the alert container from left edge. Title and description text maintain proper margins and alignment without icon spacing considerations.

2629:56948.name: Alert
2629:56948.description:
A notification container that displays important messages to users with contextual styling and optional interactive elements. The component features a rounded rectangular layout with distinct background colors corresponding to message severity levels. Each alert includes an icon on the left side that matches the variant type, a content area with title and description text, and an optional dismiss button (X) positioned in the top-right corner. The component supports multiple severity variants (success, info, warning, danger) with corresponding visual treatments and icons. Optional elements include the ability to hide the icon, remove the dismiss functionality, exclude the title, or include actionable links within the content area.


Property-Based Instance Descriptions
Variant Typessuccess: Green background with checkmark icon for positive confirmations
info: Blue background with information icon for neutral notifications
warning: Orange/yellow background with triangle warning icon for cautionary messages
danger: Red/pink background with exclamation icon for error or critical alerts
Content ConfigurationhasIcon: true: Displays variant-appropriate icon on left side of alert
hasIcon: false: Alert displays without icon, content spans full width
isDismissible: true: X button visible in top-right corner for user dismissal
isDismissible: false: No dismiss button present, alert remains persistent
hasTitle: true: Title text displayed prominently above description
hasTitle: false: Only description text shown, no title element
hasLink: true: Actionable link element included within alert content
hasLink: false: Alert contains only static text content
Text PropertiestitleText: Accepts custom text for alert heading
descriptionText: Accepts custom text for alert body content


Usage GuidelinesPrimary Use CasesForm validation feedback showing success, error, or warning states
System notifications for process completion, failures, or status updates
Contextual help messages providing information about current interface state
Marketing or promotional messages that can be dismissed by users
Critical alerts that require user acknowledgment before proceeding
Implementation ConsiderationsUse success variants for positive confirmations like successful form submissions
Apply info variants for neutral system information or helpful tips
Implement warning variants for cautionary messages that don't block user actions
Reserve danger variants for errors, failures, or critical issues requiring attention
Include dismiss functionality for non-critical alerts to improve user control
Maintain consistent messaging tone and length appropriate to alert severity
Consider alert placement and timing to avoid interrupting user workflows
Accessibility RequirementsIcons must have appropriate alt text or aria-labels describing alert type
Dismiss buttons must be keyboard accessible with clear focus indicators
Screen readers should announce alert content and severity level
Alert appearance should not rely solely on color to convey meaning

2369:3049.name: Text link
2369:3049.description:
An interactive text element that provides navigation or action functionality with optional decorative icons. The component displays as inline text with distinct visual styling to indicate its clickable nature. It supports multiple emphasis levels (strong, subtle, inverted, brand) with corresponding visual treatments and interaction states. Optional start and end icons can be positioned before and after the link text to provide additional context or visual interest. The component includes comprehensive interaction states including hover, active, focus, and disabled states for complete user experience coverage.


Property-Based Instance Descriptions
Emphasis Variationsstrong: High contrast styling for primary navigation or important actions
subtle: Reduced contrast styling for secondary links or supporting navigation
inverted: Light styling for use on dark backgrounds or colored surfaces
brand: Brand-colored styling that aligns with primary brand identity
Interactive Statesdefault: Standard display state with base styling
hover: Visual feedback when cursor is positioned over link
active: Pressed state styling during user interaction
focus: Keyboard focus indicator for accessibility navigation
disabled: Non-interactive state with reduced opacity and no click functionality
Content Configurationtext: Accepts custom text strings for link label
hasStartIcon: Optional icon positioned before link text
hasEndIcon: Optional icon positioned after link text
startIcon: Configurable icon element for start position
endIcon: Configurable icon element for end position


Usage GuidelinesPrimary Use CasesNavigation links within content areas or menus
Call-to-action links in marketing or promotional content
Reference links to external resources or documentation
In-line links within body text for additional information
Action triggers disguised as text links for subtle interactions
Implementation ConsiderationsUse strong emphasis for primary actions and important navigation
Apply subtle emphasis for secondary links that support main content
Implement inverted emphasis when placing links on colored backgrounds
Reserve brand emphasis for key conversion points or brand-related actions
Include appropriate icons when they add meaningful context
Ensure sufficient color contrast across all emphasis levels
Consider link placement and surrounding content for optimal usability
Accessibility RequirementsAll interactive states must be keyboard accessible
Focus indicators must meet visibility requirements for keyboard navigation
Link text must clearly describe destination or action
Color cannot be the only means of indicating link functionality
Icons require appropriate alt text or aria-labels when present
Disabled links should be announced as non-interactive to screen readers
